L168A: Leu168 sits on a coiled-coil helix (relSASA 0.61) packed against W169/L167. ESM-2 very strongly prefers a smaller residue here (z=+2.6); Ala relieves steric crowding against the bulky Trp169 and removes an over-sized hydrophobic side chain from a partly-exposed position, which can improve folding efficiency / reduce misfolding of the dimerization arm.
N322L: Asn322 lies in the C-terminal Tnp segment among hydrophobic neighbors (A318, V324) near P323. A buried/partially-buried Asn with an unsatisfied amide is destabilizing; both inverse-folding models (ProteinMPNN z=+? , ESM-IF strong) favor a hydrophobic Leu that packs into this pocket. Lower local pLDDT (74) means this prediction is less certain.
T152L: Thr152 (relSASA 0.26) contacts L149 and I163 in the coiled-coil core. The beta-branched Thr packs sub-optimally and buries a polar hydroxyl; Leu improves van der Waals complementarity with the L149/I163 cluster and removes a buried polar group, stabilizing the dimerization helix that organizes the composite active site.
S258L: Ser258 (relSASA 0.18) is surrounded by a hydrophobic pocket (V257, L259, Y215, V211). A buried serine in a hydrophobic cavity is thermodynamically costly; all three methods agree Leu better fills the pocket. This is the cleanest stability-gain hypothesis — improved folding stability of the DNA-binding Tnp domain often translates to higher effective enzyme concentration and activity.
H224N: His224 (relSASA 0.43) sits against A223/A225 on a Tnp helix. A partially-buried His imposes a charge/tautomer penalty; ProteinMPNN strongly prefers Asn (z=+3.2), a smaller polar residue that satisfies local H-bonding without the bulky/titratable imidazole, reducing local strain.
